Title: Proofread for grammar and spelling
Content: Proofread the following passage for grammar and spelling errors, and suggest corrections.

Title: Tighten prose without losing voice
Content: Tighten the prose in the passage to improve clarity and pace while preserving the author's voice.

Title: Check dialogue punctuation
Content: Check the dialogue punctuation and formatting in the passage and correct any issues.

Title: Find repetitive words
Content: Identify repetitive words or phrases in the passage and suggest alternatives to reduce repetition.

Title: Improve sentence variety
Content: Improve sentence variety by suggesting rephrasing to vary length and structure while maintaining meaning.

Title: Strengthen character voice
Content: Suggest revisions to strengthen and differentiate the character's voice in the passage.

Title: Add sensory details
Content: Add sensory details (sight, sound, smell, touch, taste) to the passage to make the scene more vivid.

Title: Increase tension
Content: Propose changes to increase tension and stakes in this scene.

Title: Show don't tell
Content: Rewrite sections that tell emotion or context into showing through action and sensory detail.

Title: Improve pacing
Content: Suggest edits to improve pacing, including where to expand or condense.

Title: Outline this chapter
Content: Provide a concise outline of the chapter's structure including major beats.

Title: Identify the turning point
Content: Identify and explain the chapter's turning point and ways to emphasize it.

Title: Check scene structure
Content: Assess each scene for goal, conflict, and outcome. Suggest improvements.

Title: Improve transitions
Content: Provide suggestions to improve transitions between scenes or sections.

Title: Strengthen opening hook
Content: Give options to strengthen the opening hook to better grab the reader.

Title: Clarify main argument
Content: Clarify and sharpen the main argument of this passage.

Title: Add supporting examples
Content: Suggest supporting examples or evidence to strengthen the argument.

Title: Improve readability
Content: Rewrite for improved readability for a general audience.

Title: Strengthen conclusion
Content: Propose ways to strengthen the conclusion and leave a lasting impression.

Title: Check logical flow
Content: Check for logical gaps and suggest restructuring for clearer argument flow.

Title: Fact-check this passage
Content: Identify factual claims in the passage and suggest ways to verify them.

Title: Suggest credible sources
Content: Recommend credible primary and secondary sources related to the passage.

Title: Identify unsupported claims
Content: Highlight claims that lack support and recommend evidence to add.

Title: Add citations
Content: Suggest appropriate citation formats and likely sources for the claims.

Title: Verify historical accuracy
Content: Check the passage for historical accuracy and flag potential anachronisms.

Title: Expand with sensory details
Content: Take this passage and expand it with vivid sensory details. Add what the character sees, hears, smells, feels, and tastes. Make the reader feel present in the scene without slowing the pacing.

Title: Describe the setting
Content: Describe this location in rich detail. Include the mood, lighting, sounds, smells, and textures. Show how the setting reflects the character's emotional state or the story's themes.

Title: Add metaphors and similes
Content: Enhance this passage with fresh metaphors and similes. Make abstract concepts concrete. Avoid clichés. Use comparisons that fit the character's voice and the story's tone.

Title: Deepen the atmosphere
Content: Intensify the atmosphere in this scene. If it's tense, make it more tense. If it's romantic, make it more romantic. If it's eerie, make it more unsettling. Use environmental details and subtext.

Title: Show character through environment
Content: Reveal character personality through their interaction with the setting. Show what they notice, ignore, or obsess over. Let the environment reflect their internal state.

Title: Create vivid action
Content: Rewrite this action sequence with sharp, visceral details. Vary sentence length for rhythm. Include physical sensations. Make every movement count. Keep it clear despite the intensity.

Title: Write the weather
Content: Add weather to this scene that enhances the mood. Don't just describe it — show how characters interact with it. Use weather to reflect or contrast the emotional tone.

Title: Describe a character's appearance
Content: Introduce this character's appearance through action and other characters' reactions, not a mirror or static description. Reveal details that matter to the story or characterization.

Title: Build tension through setting
Content: Use the physical environment to build tension. Add ominous details, uncomfortable spaces, or threatening elements. Make the setting feel unstable or dangerous.

Title: Paint the emotional landscape
Content: Describe this moment through the character's emotional filter. Let their feelings color how they perceive the world. Show the scene as they experience it, not objectively.

Title: Sharpen the dialogue
Content: Tighten this dialogue. Cut filler words, make it sound natural, add subtext. Each line should reveal character or advance the plot. Remove exposition disguised as conversation.

Title: Add conflict to conversation
Content: Rewrite this dialogue with more tension. Give characters opposing goals. Add interruptions, evasions, or misunderstandings. Show the power dynamic shifting.

Title: Differentiate character voices
Content: Rewrite this conversation so each character has a distinct voice. Consider vocabulary, sentence structure, rhythm, formality, humor, and verbal tics. Make them sound like different people.

Title: Write subtext
Content: Add a layer of subtext to this dialogue. Characters should say one thing but mean another. Show what they're not saying through body language, pauses, and what they avoid.

Title: Show emotion through dialogue
Content: Rewrite this conversation to show the character's emotional state through their speech patterns. Angry people speak differently than anxious people. Let emotion affect word choice and rhythm.

Title: Write realistic argument
Content: Turn this into a realistic argument. Include interruptions, raised voices, low blows, defensive reactions, and the messy back-and-forth of real conflict. Make it uncomfortable but believable.

Title: Add dialogue beats
Content: Add action beats to this dialogue that reveal character and emotion. Show what characters do while talking. Use physical reactions to emphasize or contradict their words.

Title: Write witty banter
Content: Enhance this exchange with clever wordplay, callbacks, and quick wit. Keep it natural to the characters. Banter should reveal relationship dynamics and personality.

Title: Craft a confession or revelation
Content: Write this moment where a character reveals something important. Build tension before the reveal. Show their reluctance or urgency. Capture the other character's reaction.

Title: Improve exposition in dialogue
Content: Take this exposition and make it feel natural in conversation. Characters should reveal information organically, with emotion and conflict, not just info-dumping.

Title: Brainstorm plot twists
Content: Based on what's happened so far, suggest 5 unexpected but logical plot twists that could occur next. Each should complicate the protagonist's goal and raise the stakes.

Title: Find the story stakes
Content: Analyze this passage. What does the protagonist want? What will happen if they fail? Are the stakes clear and compelling? Suggest ways to raise the stakes.

Title: Create obstacles
Content: The protagonist wants their goal. Brainstorm 10 obstacles that could prevent them from achieving it. Include internal conflicts, external forces, and complications from other characters.

Title: Write the next scene
Content: Based on this passage, outline what should happen in the next scene. Consider the protagonist's goal, current conflicts, and story momentum. Suggest scene goals and turning points.

Title: Reverse engineer the climax
Content: This is the current state of the story. Working backward, suggest what needs to happen to build toward a satisfying climax. What conflicts need resolution? What revelations are needed?

Title: Add a subplot
Content: Suggest a subplot that could weave through this main story. It should echo the theme, involve secondary characters, and intersect with the main plot at key moments.

Title: Strengthen the inciting incident
Content: Analyze this opening. Is the inciting incident clear and compelling? Does it disrupt the protagonist's normal world? Suggest ways to make it more dramatic or consequential.

Title: Build to a cliffhanger
Content: Turn the end of this chapter into a cliffhanger. Raise a question, introduce a complication, or reveal something shocking. Make the reader need to continue.

Title: Create cause and effect
Content: Check the logic chain: Does each event naturally lead to the next? Tighten the cause-and-effect relationship. Remove coincidences. Show clear connections between actions and consequences.

Title: Map character arcs
Content: Based on this passage, map out where this character started and where they're going. What internal change needs to happen? Suggest key moments that will drive their transformation.

Title: Deepen character motivation
Content: Analyze this character's actions. What do they really want? Why do they want it? Dig beneath surface desires to find deeper psychological needs. Show how past experiences shaped this motivation.

Title: Create character contradictions
Content: Add complexity by giving this character contradictory traits. Show them being brave and cowardly, kind and cruel, confident and insecure in different contexts. Make them inconsistent like real people.

Title: Write an internal monologue
Content: Show this character's thoughts during this moment. Reveal their fears, desires, rationalizations, and mental commentary. Let their inner voice contrast with what they say and do externally.

Title: Develop character relationships
Content: Show the dynamic between these two characters. What's their history? Power balance? Unspoken tensions? How do they bring out different sides of each other?

Title: Give the character a secret
Content: This character is hiding something. What is it? How does it affect their behavior? Show moments where they almost reveal it or work hard to conceal it.

Title: Show character growth
Content: Show how this character has changed since the beginning. What beliefs have shifted? What can they do now that they couldn't before? Make the transformation visible in this scene.

Title: Create a character quirk or habit
Content: Give this character a distinctive quirk, habit, or verbal tic that reveals personality. It should be specific and meaningful, not random. Show it in action.

Title: Explore character backstory
Content: Write a brief backstory moment that explains why this character acts this way. Show a formative experience. Make it relevant to the current story conflict.

Title: Test character values
Content: Put this character in a situation where they must choose between two things they value. Force them to reveal their priorities through action. Show the internal struggle.

Title: Write character reactions
Content: How would this specific character react to the situation at hand? Show their unique response based on their personality, history, and current emotional state. Avoid generic reactions.

Title: Write a compelling opening line
Content: Craft an opening sentence that immediately grabs attention. It should establish voice, hint at conflict, and make the reader want to know more. Avoid clichés and weather descriptions.

Title: Strengthen the first paragraph
Content: Rewrite this opening paragraph to hook the reader immediately. Start with action, voice, or intrigue. Establish tone and stakes. Make every sentence earn its place.

Title: Open with dialogue
Content: Begin this scene with a striking line of dialogue that reveals character, conflict, or situation. Follow with context. Make the reader need to know who's speaking and why.

Title: Start in medias res
Content: Drop the reader into the middle of the action. Begin with the protagonist already facing a problem or making a choice. Fill in context as you go.

Title: Create mystery in the opening
Content: Write an opening that raises questions. What's strange? What's missing? What doesn't add up? Make the reader need answers.

Title: Write a satisfying chapter ending
Content: End this chapter in a way that provides some closure while propelling the story forward. Resolve the immediate scene goal while raising new questions.

Title: Craft an emotional ending
Content: Write the emotional resolution for this character's arc. Show the transformation, the acceptance, the loss, or the victory. Make the reader feel it.

Title: Stick the landing
Content: Write the final paragraph of this story. Resolve the central question while leaving room for interpretation. End with an image or line that resonates.

Title: Write a circular ending
Content: Connect the ending back to the opening. Echo an image, phrase, or situation from the beginning, but show how everything has changed.

Title: Create bittersweet resolution
Content: Not all endings are happy. Write a conclusion that mixes victory and loss, growth and sacrifice. Show the cost of the protagonist's journey.
